IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
These works were originally scheduled for the 9th February 2026 but were postponed due to unforeseen site restrictions. To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of trees near our overhead cables. This has identified trees that have grown close, which could damage the cables and affect your electricity supply.
 Our contractors need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut the trees away from the cables. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.
 This work is taking place at the rear of King Business Centre on Reeds Lane.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place on land off Grove
Road.Please note these works were originally scheduled for the 25th February 2026 but were cancelled due to
unforeseen resourcing issues. To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular
inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified a piece of equipment called a transformer and an
electricity pole that needs replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power
cut in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.
To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off and upgrade the transformer and
replace the electricity pole. We will remove the existing transformer and install a new one on the
same day.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place near Lion Road and Old Bury Road Area.To improve the reliability of your electricity supply, our engineers will be carrying out upgrades to the local network. This work
includes converting part of the high-voltage network to a three-phase system and installing a new transformer, following a
customer request. While you are connected to the generator our engineers will also take the opportunity to replace several
electricity poles in the area.
Once the work is completed, this will improve the reliability of our electricity network and provide the third party with their desired
outcome. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the checks. To
connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 07:10 - We can now confirm that all power in the Parkville Road area should now be back on.

Upon investigation, the issue was found to be a problem with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now repaired. Although there were power cuts some weeks ago, now that our engineers have rectified this, we're pleased to say we do not believe it will cause any subsequent issues with your power.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the recent outages. If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now arrived on site in the Parkville Road area.

As any issues that currently exist on our network have affected our equipment at the substation, engineers will start their testing there. They will then check our network equipment throughout the area to try and identify any problems, before trying to restore power.

Our estimated time frame of 09:00 and 10:00 hasn't changed, but if this is not successful it's possible that a cable repair may be needed. This can involve ground excavations, traffic management equipment or specialist teams. As soon as our staff can safely update us, we'll give you the latest news. Sent by Roland at 06:39.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 05:48 - Our staff are on the way to the Parkville Road area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 09:00 and 10:00. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. Developing faults can start very small, and so can be hard to detect but each power cut usually makes it easier to see, which means our specialist staff can detect it, and work on the repair to prevent further outages.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected high voltage network fault that affected 993 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 993 customers in the Huntingdon, PE28 area by diverting power around a fault where the overhead and underground networks meet.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve the issue safely. We apologise the outage lasted longer than anticipated and we appreciate your understanding.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Stuart at 15:13.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Our engineers have let us know that they are still in the process of pinpointing where the fault lies on the overhead network to then carry out repairs. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 15:30 and 16:30.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We were investigating a high voltage overhead electricity line fault which caused a power cut in the local area.

Logs.Comment:
Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected high voltage network fault that affected 993 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible.

Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms

I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.

Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 993 customers in the Huntingdon, PE28 area by diverting power around a fault where the overhead and underground networks meet.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve the issue safely. We apologise the outage lasted longer than anticipated and we appreciate your understanding.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Stuart at 15:13.

Sent by Stuart at 14:09 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that they are still in the process of pinpointing where the fault lies on the overhead network to then carry out repairs. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 15:30 and 16:30.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 12:11 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Engineers have arrived on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the Ferry Lane area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 15:00 and 16:00, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but for more information about this type of fault; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Adam at 11:49.
